_notes: Auto-generated by scripts/classify_districts.py (Stage 4) produced an EMPTY rule set: the pack's scraped breadcrumbs are only 2 levels deep (chapter 'Zoning' -> article, e.g. 'DISTRICT REGULATIONS'), with no chapter-level breakdown, so Stage 4's article-level grouping lumped all of Staunton City Code Title 18's per-district chapters (167 sections) into one heterogeneous 'DISTRICT REGULATIONS' bucket and correctly refused to assign it a single district. Human-reviewed 2026-07-19: like lynchburg-va and salem-va (not fredericksburg-va or manassas-va), Staunton gives EACH district its own SCC chapter of numbered sections (e.g. Chapter 18.25 = R-2: 18.25.010 General description, .020 Permitted uses, .040 Area regulations) whose titles never repeat the district code, so this pack uses the numeric-prefix keying strategy: article_contains='DISTRICT REGULATIONS' (breadcrumb[1], read from source.metadata['breadcrumb'] at runtime per app/source_classifier.py -- this pack's top-level manifest 'article' fields are always null, like salem-va) plus title_contains='18.XX.' per district chapter. Chapter-to-district identities confirmed by reading each chapter's 'General description' full_text against the official 17-classification table in SCC 18.15.010 (A-1/R-1/R-2/R-3/R-4/R-5/R-6/P-1/B-1/B-2/B-3/B-4/H-1/HE-1/I-1/I-2/I-3; TND-I, B-5 and the 2024 small-lot district in Ch. 18.47 post-date that table) and against the city's live ArcGIS zoning layer (Zoning FeatureServer at services.arcgis.com/bp3MWUdz4OBPF6xz/arcgis/rest/services/Zoning/FeatureServer/0, field LABEL_FLD, which maps B-1/B-2/B-3/B-5/HE-1/I-1/I-2/I-3c/P-1/R-1/R-2/R-3/R-4/TNDIc/TRD-1 polygons -- identifying Ch. 18.47's small-lot district as TRD-1 'Traditional Residential Development District'; R-5/R-6/B-4/A-1 currently have no polygons in that layer). Mapping rationale: (1) 18.20 R-1, 18.25 R-2 (single-family), 18.30 R-3 (medium incl. multi-family), 18.35 R-4 (high-density multi-family), 18.40 R-5 (manufactured home park), 18.45 R-6 (manufactured home subdivision), 18.47 TRD-1 (small-lot single-family) -> all residential-low-density, the same all-residential collapse as lynchburg R-* and salem RSF/RMF/MHP. (2) 18.95 A-1 agricultural-forestal districts (Va. Agricultural and Forestal Districts Act, 18.95.030 carries the district's required/permitted uses) -> the dedicated 'agricultural' term per the manassas A-1 / salem AG precedent. (3) JUDGMENT CALLS to mixed-use-core: 18.50 P-1 Professional (permits ANY R-4 use by right plus offices/medical, and living quarters in the same building as professional uses -- real mixed by-right uses, the salem RB precedent), 18.55 B-1 Local business (upper-floor living/sleeping quarters by right above neighborhood retail, 18.55.020(1)), 18.60 B-2 General business (inherits every B-1 use incl. upper-floor dwellings, and is the district of downtown Staunton's central business district per the assessor's 'CEN BUS DIST' neighborhood coding), 18.53 TND-I (literally 'development of a mixed-use traditional neighborhood'), 18.71 B-5 (intent text 'encourage development of mixed land uses'; permits any B-2 use plus any R-4 use). (4) commercial-employment: 18.65 B-3 Planned business (B-1 use list but everything only on planning-commission+council review, suburban planned centers -- no by-right residential), 18.70 B-4 Cultural and recreational planned business, 18.90 HE-1 Higher education (campus/institutional, the lynchburg IN-1/IN-2 / salem CUD institutional precedent). (5) industrial-zone: 18.75 I-1 Light industrial (18.75.020(2) expressly bars residential use), 18.80 I-2 Heavy industrial, 18.100 I-3 Planned industrial. Deliberately left UNMAPPED (fall through to unknown/general): Ch. 18.83 Entrance corridor overlay (an overlay spanning underlying base districts) and Ch. 18.85 H-1 Historic preservation (procedural: commission creation, certificate-of-appropriateness process; H-1 overlays other base districts rather than carrying its own use list).
